subject: English

lesson.story_mission: Join the Opinion Orchard crew to turn clear ideas into Grade 2 paragraphs.
lesson.objective: I can state an opinion, support it with reasons, connect my ideas, and write a conclusion.
lesson.concept_explanation: An opinion tells what a writer thinks. A strong opinion paragraph names the topic, states the opinion, gives connected reasons, uses simple linking words, and ends with a conclusion.
lesson.common_misconception: A fact about the topic is not always a reason. A reason must explain why the opinion makes sense.
lesson.real_world_connection: Opinion writing helps you explain choices about books, school ideas, activities, and your community.
lesson.lesson_summary: State what you think, support it with connected reasons, and close by returning to your opinion.
lesson.mini_lesson: Ask: What do I think? Why do I think it? Then link each reason to the opinion and finish with a conclusion.

worked_examples.text: Opinion: Our class should grow herbs. Reason: Caring for herbs helps us learn how plants grow. Conclusion: That is why an herb garden would help our class.
worked_examples.correct_answer: The reason explains a learning benefit that supports the opinion.

worked_examples.text: Fact: The gym has two doors. Opinion: Gym class is an important part of the day.
worked_examples.correct_answer: The fact can be checked; the opinion tells a judgment.

hint_ladder: Name the opinion-writing job you need to do.
Connect each reason or sentence to the writer’s opinion.
Reread the completed writing to check focus and order.

guided_practice.prompt: Which sentence is an opinion about the school playground?
guided_practice.correct_answer: The playground is the most exciting place at school.
hints: Look for words that show a feeling or judgment about school playground.
Ask whether everyone must agree or whether the statement can be checked.
Reread your choice and decide: fact that can be proved, or opinion someone may debate?
guided_practice.explanation: The word most shows what the writer thinks; another person could prefer a different place.
guided_practice.feedback: Reread the explanation to see how the writing choice works.
choices: The playground is the most exciting place at school.
Our class visits the playground at noon.

question_audio.text: Which sentence is an opinion about the school playground?
question_audio.label: Read Question

audio.text: The playground is the most exciting place at school.
audio.label: Hear the answer

guided_practice.prompt: Which sentence is a fact rather than an opinion?
guided_practice.correct_answer: An apple has seeds inside it.
hints: Look for words that show a feeling or judgment about apples.
Ask whether everyone must agree or whether the statement can be checked.
Reread your choice and decide: fact that can be proved, or opinion someone may debate?
guided_practice.explanation: Having seeds is something that can be checked, so the sentence is a fact rather than a personal judgment.
guided_practice.feedback: Reread the explanation to see how the writing choice works.
choices: Every lunch should include an apple.
An apple has seeds inside it.
Red apples are the prettiest fruit.

question_audio.text: Which sentence is a fact rather than an opinion?
question_audio.label: Read Question

audio.text: An apple has seeds inside it.
audio.label: Hear the answer

guided_practice.prompt: Nora is writing about classroom jobs. Which sentence clearly states her opinion?
guided_practice.correct_answer: Passing out papers is the best classroom job.
hints: Look for words that show a feeling or judgment about classroom jobs.
Ask whether everyone must agree or whether the statement can be checked.
Reread your choice and decide: fact that can be proved, or opinion someone may debate?
guided_practice.explanation: Calling one job the best tells Nora’s judgment and gives her opinion a clear focus.
guided_practice.feedback: Reread the explanation to see how the writing choice works.
choices: Our job chart hangs by the door.
There are five jobs on Monday.
Passing out papers is the best classroom job.

question_audio.text: Nora is writing about classroom jobs. Which sentence clearly states her opinion?
question_audio.label: Read Question

audio.text: Passing out papers is the best classroom job.
audio.label: Hear the answer

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Which sentence tells an opinion about reading outdoors?
adaptive_question_bank.correct_answer: Reading outdoors is more peaceful than reading inside.
hints: Look for words that show a feeling or judgment about outdoor reading.
Ask whether everyone must agree or whether the statement can be checked.
Reread your choice and decide: fact that can be proved, or opinion someone may debate?
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: More peaceful is a judgment that readers may disagree about, so it states an opinion.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Reread the explanation to see how the writing choice works.
choices: Reading outdoors is more peaceful than reading inside.
Our class read outdoors on Friday.
We carried books to the garden.

question_audio.text: Which sentence tells an opinion about reading outdoors?
question_audio.label: Read Question

audio.text: Reading outdoors is more peaceful than reading inside.
audio.label: Hear the answer

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Which statement could be used as an opinion in a paragraph about lunch?
adaptive_question_bank.correct_answer: Warm soup is the coziest lunch on a cold day.
hints: Look for words that show a feeling or judgment about cold-day lunch.
Ask whether everyone must agree or whether the statement can be checked.
Reread your choice and decide: fact that can be proved, or opinion someone may debate?
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: Coziest tells how the writer feels and gives a clear opinion to support with reasons.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Reread the explanation to see how the writing choice works.
choices: Soup is served in a bowl.
Warm soup is the coziest lunch on a cold day.

question_audio.text: Which statement could be used as an opinion in a paragraph about lunch?
question_audio.label: Read Question

audio.text: Warm soup is the coziest lunch on a cold day.
audio.label: Hear the answer

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Which sentence can be proven true?
adaptive_question_bank.correct_answer: A snowflake is made of frozen water.
hints: Look for words that show a feeling or judgment about snowflakes.
Ask whether everyone must agree or whether the statement can be checked.
Reread your choice and decide: fact that can be proved, or opinion someone may debate?
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: This information can be checked in a reliable source, which makes it a fact.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Reread the explanation to see how the writing choice works.
choices: Snow is the most beautiful weather.
A snowflake is made of frozen water.
Winter should have more snow days.
Building a snow fort is always fun.

audio.text: A snowflake is made of frozen water.

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: A writer says, “Board games are better than video games.” What kind of statement is this?
hints: Look for words that show a feeling or judgment about board games.
Ask whether everyone must agree or whether the statement can be checked.
Reread your choice and decide: fact that can be proved, or opinion someone may debate?
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: The word better compares two choices according to the writer’s judgment, so the statement is an opinion.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Reread the explanation to see how the writing choice works.

question_audio.text: A writer says, “Board games are better than video games.” What kind of statement is this?
question_audio.label: Read Question

audio.text: an opinion
audio.label: Hear the answer

adaptive_question_bank.prompt: Our class should take a short movement break because ___.
adaptive_question_bank.correct_answer: moving helps students return to work ready to focus.
hints: Name the opinion about movement breaks before looking for a reason.
Choose a detail that answers why that opinion makes sense.
Join the opinion and reason with because, then reread to check that the ideas connect.
adaptive_question_bank.explanation: This reason directly explains a real benefit of movement breaks; it gives the reader support for the opinion.
adaptive_question_bank.feedback: Reread the explanation to see how the writing choice works.
acceptable_answers: moving helps students return to work ready to focus.

question_audio.text: Our class should take a short movement break because ___.
question_audio.label: Read Question

audio.text: moving helps students return to work ready to focus.
audio.label: Hear the answer

game_theme.title: Write Opinion Pieces Skill World
game_theme.mission_intro: Build writing power by practicing state an opinion, give reasons, and provide a closing.

questions.prompt: Write a three-sentence opinion paragraph about rain boots. Include an opinion, one supporting reason, and a conclusion.

audio.label: Hear the answer
